rules.title: Verb Second (V2)
rules.formula: Position 1 + finite verb + remainder of clause
rules.description: In a normal statement, the conjugated verb is always the second element. The first slot can be the subject, a time word, or another starter.
rules.description_cn: 陈述句中，变位动词通常位于第二位；第一位可以是主语、时间词或其他成分。

examples.en: The child plays in the park.

rules.title: Yes–No Questions
rules.description: Yes/No questions place the finite verb in first position, then the subject.
rules.description_cn: 一般疑问句把变位动词放在句首，然后是主语。

examples.en: Does the child have a book?

rules.title: W-Questions
rules.description: The question word comes first, then the verb, then the subject in simple A1 questions.
rules.description_cn: 特殊疑问句中疑问词在前，然后是动词和主语。

rules.title: Conjunctions (und, oder, aber)
rules.description: und adds information, oder offers a choice, aber shows a small contrast. Each clause keeps normal word order at A1.
rules.description_cn: und 表并列，oder 表选择，aber 表转折；A1 阶段每个分句仍保持基本语序。

examples.de: Das Buch ist klein, aber gut.
examples.en: The book is small, but good.

rules.title: Imperative
rules.description: Use the imperative for short commands. Formal Sie forms are common in polite travel or class phrases.
rules.description_cn: 命令式用于简短指令；正式场合常用 Sie 形式。

rules.title: Present Tense — Regular Verbs
rules.description: Drop -en from the infinitive, add the present endings for each person.
rules.description_cn: 去掉不定词尾 -en 得到词干，再按人称加现在时词尾。

examples.de: Ich lerne Deutsch. Du lernst Deutsch.
examples.en: I learn German. You learn German.

rules.title: Present Tense — Irregular Verbs
rules.formula: Same persons as regular verbs; stem or vowel may change
rules.description: haben and sein are essential irregular verbs. Some verbs change a vowel in du and er/sie/es (e.g. essen, fahren).
rules.description_cn: haben、sein 为不规则动词；部分动词在 du/er 等形式中元音变化（如 essen、fahren）。

examples.de: Ich bin müde. Du bist hier.
examples.en: I am tired. You are here.

examples.de: Er hat ein Buch. Wir haben Zeit.
examples.en: He has a book. We have time.

examples.de: Du isst Brot. Sie fährt mit der Bahn.
examples.en: You eat bread. She travels by train.

rules.title: Modal Verbs
rules.formula: Subject + modal (finite) + … + infinitive at the end
rules.description: Modals express ability, obligation, or desire. The main verb stays in the infinitive at the end in simple A1 sentences.
rules.description_cn: 情态动词表示能力、义务或意愿；主要动词以不定式形式放在句末。

rules.title: Separable Verbs
rules.formula: Prefix + verb → finite verb in V2, prefix often at clause end
rules.description: Many verbs have a separable prefix. In present statements, the conjugated verb stands in second position and the prefix moves to the end.
rules.description_cn: 可分动词的前缀在陈述句中常置于句末，变位动词仍占第二位。

rules.title: Gender (der, die, das)
rules.formula: der + masculine, die + feminine, das + neuter
rules.description: Every noun has a gender. Learn the article together with the noun.
rules.description_cn: 德语名词有性，需与冠词一起记忆。

examples.de: der Mann, die Frau, das Kind
examples.en: the man, the woman, the child

examples.de: der Tag, die Nacht, das Jahr
examples.en: the day, the night, the year

rules.title: Definite and Indefinite Articles
rules.description: Use definite articles when the thing is known or specific; use ein/eine like “a/an” when it is new or general.
rules.description_cn: 定冠词表已知或特定事物；不定冠词类似英语 a/an。

rules.title: Plural Forms
rules.formula: Plural noun + plural article die (core A1 pattern)
rules.description: Common plural markers include -e, -en, -er, -n, or -s. Learn plural forms with the noun.
rules.description_cn: 复数常有 -e、-en、-er、-n、-s 等标记，需与名词一并记忆。

rules.title: Negation (kein and nicht)
rules.formula: kein + noun; nicht before verb, adjective, or focused part
rules.description: kein replaces ein/eine before a noun you negate. nicht negates verbs, adjectives, or whole clauses.
rules.description_cn: kein 用于否定带不定冠词的名词；nicht 否定动词、形容词或整个命题焦点。

rules.title: Nominative Case
rules.description: The subject is in the nominative. It answers “who or what is acting?”
rules.description_cn: 主语用第一格，表示“谁/什么”发出动作。

rules.title: Accusative Case
rules.description: The accusative marks the direct object. Masculine articles change: der → den, ein → einen. Feminine and neuter articles often stay the same in many A1 patterns.
rules.description_cn: 第四格表直接宾语；阳性冠词 der→den，ein→einen；阴性和中性在许多 A1 句型中形式不变。

rules.title: Personal Pronouns
rules.formula: Nominative for subject; accusative for direct object
rules.description: Personal pronouns replace nouns. Learn nominative and accusative forms for people and things you talk about every day.
rules.description_cn: 人称代词代替名词；需掌握主格与宾格的基本形式。

rules.title: Possessive Determiners
rules.formula: Possessive + noun (endings follow A1 patterns you learn in class)
rules.description: mein, dein, sein, ihr show ownership. Endings agree with the following noun in gender and number.
rules.description_cn: mein、dein、sein、ihr 等表示所属，词尾随后面名词的性与数变化。

rules.title: Time Prepositions (am, um, im)
rules.formula: am + weekday; um + clock time; im + month/season
rules.description: Use these for simple plans. Keep verbs in the present tense at A1.
rules.description_cn: 表示星期用 am，钟点用 um，月份/季节用 im；A1 多用现在时。

examples.en: At eight o'clock I eat breakfast.

examples.de: Im Sommer fahren wir in die Stadt.
examples.en: In summer we go to the city.

rules.title: Place Prepositions (aus, in, nach)
rules.formula: aus + origin; in + place; nach + city/country (motion)
rules.description: aus marks where someone comes from. in marks being inside a place. nach marks movement toward a city or country in basic travel phrases.
rules.description_cn: aus 表来源；in 表在某地；nach 表前往城市或国家（基础位移用法）。
